text: NPS (National Pension System) is open to all Indian citizens between the ages of 18 and 70 years. This includes salaried individuals, self-employed professionals, and business owners. Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) and Overseas Citizenship of India (OCI) holders are also eligible to open an NPS Tier I account, subject to applicable residency norms and FEMA compliance. The minimum age of 18 ensures young earners can start building their retirement corpus early, while the upper limit of 70 allows late entrants and those returning from abroad to participate. The account remains active until the subscriber exits or reaches maturity age.
textHindi: NPS 18 se 70 saal ke Bharatiya nagrikoṃ ke liye khula hai. NRI aur OCI cardholders bhi Tier I khata khol sakte hain, lekin FEMA niyamon ka paalan zaroori hai.
embedding_hint: Who is eligible to open an NPS account? Age limit for NPS. Can NRIs open NPS?

text: NRIs and OCI card holders are allowed to open and operate only an NPS Tier I account. They are not permitted to open a Tier II account unless they become Indian residents again. All contributions must comply with FEMA (Foreign Exchange Management Act) regulations. NRI subscribers must ensure that contributions are made through NRE or NRO accounts as applicable. If an NRI subscriber's citizenship status changes or they become a PIO (Person of Indian Origin), they must notify PFRDA. Repatriation of NPS proceeds is subject to prevailing FEMA rules at the time of withdrawal or exit. OCI holders should also verify their country's tax treaty with India before contributing, as double taxation may apply on annuity income.
textHindi: NRI aur OCI sirf Tier I account khol sakte hain. Tier II unke liye available nahi hai. FEMA niyamon ka paalan karna zaroori hai. NRE ya NRO account se contribution hona chahiye.
embedding_hint: NRI NPS account rules. Can OCI holders open NPS? FEMA compliance for NPS contributions.

text: All Central Government employees who joined service on or after January 1, 2004, are mandatorily covered under NPS, replacing the Old Pension Scheme (OPS). Their NPS contribution is automatically deducted from their salary each month. The employee contributes 10% of basic pay plus DA, and the government (employer) also contributes 14% of basic pay plus DA to the employee's Tier I account. State government employees are also covered under NPS in most states, though enrollment dates vary by state. Defence personnel are exempt in some cases. The auto-enrollment means no separate registration is required for new government joiners — their HR or Pay & Accounts Office handles PRAN generation.
textHindi: 2004 ke baad join karne wale sarkaari karmchari NPS mein auto-enrolled hote hain. 10% employee + 14% employer contribution hota hai. Salary se automatic deduction hoti hai.
embedding_hint: Is NPS mandatory for government employees? When did NPS start for govt employees? Government employee NPS contribution percentage.

text: 2004 ke baad join karne wale sarkaari karmchari NPS mein auto-enrolled hote hain. 10% employee + 14% employer contribution hota hai. Salary se automatic deduction hoti hai.
textHindi: 2004 ke baad join karne wale sarkaari karmchari NPS mein auto-enrolled hote hain. 10% employee + 14% employer contribution hota hai. Salary se automatic deduction hoti hai.
embedding_hint: Is NPS mandatory for government employees? When did NPS start for govt employees? Government employee NPS contribution percentage.

text: Private sector employees and self-employed individuals can voluntarily enroll in NPS. There is no employer mandate in the private sector — joining is entirely at the individual's discretion. Enrollment can be done online through the eNPS portal at enps.nsdl.com using Aadhaar OTP for a fully paperless process, or through a registered Point of Presence (PoP) agent such as a bank branch. Employers in the private sector may optionally contribute to their employees' NPS on a voluntary basis, in which case the employer's contribution qualifies for deduction under Section 80CCD(2). Self-employed individuals can contribute based on gross income rather than salary. There is no restriction on profession or income level for voluntary enrollment.
textHindi: Niji sector ke log eNPS portal (enps.nsdl.com) ya PoP agent ke zariye NPS join kar sakte hain. Self-employed bhi join kar sakte hain. Employer ka contribution optional hai.
embedding_hint: How can private sector employees join NPS? How to enroll in NPS online? Can self-employed join NPS?

embedding_hint: How can private sector employees join NPS? How to enroll in NPS online? Can self-employed join NPS?

text: For NPS Tier I, the minimum contribution per transaction is Rs. 500. The minimum annual contribution required to keep the account active is Rs. 1,000 per financial year. If the annual minimum is not met, the account becomes dormant and a penalty of Rs. 100 per year of default is charged at the time of reactivation. There is no maximum limit on contributions to NPS — you can invest as much as you wish. For Tier II accounts, the minimum initial contribution is Rs. 1,000 and subsequent contributions must be at least Rs. 250. Contributions can be made anytime — monthly, quarterly, lump sum, or whenever convenient. There is no fixed SIP date requirement for voluntary contributors.
textHindi: Tier I mein har baar kam se kam Rs. 500 aur saalana kam se kam Rs. 1,000 dena zaroori hai. Nahi dene par account dormant ho jata hai aur Rs. 100 penalty lagti hai. Maximum limit nahi hai.
keywords: how much to invest in nps
embedding_hint: What is the minimum contribution for NPS? What happens if I don't contribute to NPS? Maximum NPS contribution limit.

textHindi: Tier I mein har baar kam se kam Rs. 500 aur saalana kam se kam Rs. 1,000 dena zaroori hai. Nahi dene par account dormant ho jata hai aur Rs. 100 penalty lagti hai. Maximum limit nahi hai.

text: Section 80CCD(1) of the Income Tax Act provides a tax deduction on your own (employee's) NPS contribution. For salaried employees, the deduction is limited to 10% of salary (Basic + DA). For self-employed individuals, it is 20% of gross total income. This deduction falls within the overall ceiling of Rs. 1.5 lakh per year under Section 80C. This means if you have already used your Rs. 1.5 lakh limit through PPF, ELSS, life insurance premium, or other 80C instruments, the 80CCD(1) benefit is effectively exhausted. NPS contribution under 80CCD(1) does not give you extra room beyond 1.5 lakh on its own — it competes with other 80C instruments for the same limit.
textHindi: Section 80CCD(1) ke tahat apna NPS contribution: salaried ke liye 10% salary, self-employed ke liye 20% gross income. Ye Rs. 1.5 lakh ke 80C limit ke andar aata hai.
embedding_hint: NPS tax deduction under 80CCD(1). How much deduction on own NPS contribution? NPS within 80C limit.

text: Section 80CCD(1) ke tahat apna NPS contribution: salaried ke liye 10% salary, self-employed ke liye 20% gross income. Ye Rs. 1.5 lakh ke 80C limit ke andar aata hai.
textHindi: Section 80CCD(1) ke tahat apna NPS contribution: salaried ke liye 10% salary, self-employed ke liye 20% gross income. Ye Rs. 1.5 lakh ke 80C limit ke andar aata hai.
embedding_hint: NPS tax deduction under 80CCD(1). How much deduction on own NPS contribution? NPS within 80C limit.

text: Section 80CCD(1B) is one of the most powerful tax benefits available exclusively for NPS. It allows an ADDITIONAL deduction of up to Rs. 50,000 per year for NPS contributions, completely OUTSIDE and OVER AND ABOVE the Section 80C ceiling of Rs. 1.5 lakh. This means a taxpayer can potentially claim a total deduction of Rs. 2 lakh (Rs. 1.5 lakh under 80C + Rs. 50,000 under 80CCD(1B)) by investing in NPS. No other savings or investment instrument — not PPF, not ELSS, not life insurance — qualifies for this exclusive Rs. 50,000 deduction. This benefit applies only to Tier I NPS contributions. It is not available under the New Tax Regime from FY 2023-24 onwards.
textHindi: Section 80CCD(1B) ke tahat sirf NPS ke liye extra Rs. 50,000 deduction milta hai. Ye 80C ke Rs. 1.5 lakh se alag hai. Total Rs. 2 lakh tak ka deduction sambhav hai.
embedding_hint: Extra Rs. 50,000 NPS tax deduction under 80CCD(1B). NPS deduction over and above 80C limit. Total NPS tax benefit.

text: Section 80CCD(1B) ke tahat sirf NPS ke liye extra Rs. 50,000 deduction milta hai. Ye 80C ke Rs. 1.5 lakh se alag hai. Total Rs. 2 lakh tak ka deduction sambhav hai.
textHindi: Section 80CCD(1B) ke tahat sirf NPS ke liye extra Rs. 50,000 deduction milta hai. Ye 80C ke Rs. 1.5 lakh se alag hai. Total Rs. 2 lakh tak ka deduction sambhav hai.
embedding_hint: Extra Rs. 50,000 NPS tax deduction under 80CCD(1B). NPS deduction over and above 80C limit. Total NPS tax benefit.

text: Section 80CCD(2) covers the employer's contribution to an employee's NPS Tier I account. For Central and State Government employees, the employer's NPS contribution is deductible up to 14% of basic salary + DA. For private sector employees, the deductible limit is 10% of basic salary + DA. This deduction is available to the employee — meaning the employer's contribution on the employee's behalf is not treated as taxable income for the employee, up to the specified limit. This benefit is available under both the Old Tax Regime and the New Tax Regime (since it is the only NPS-related section still applicable under the new regime). This makes NPS particularly attractive for salaried individuals whose employers contribute to NPS.
textHindi: Section 80CCD(2) mein employer ka NPS contribution: sarkaari employees ke liye 14%, niji employees ke liye 10% basic+DA tak deduction milta hai. Ye dono Old aur New Tax Regime mein available hai.
embedding_hint: Employer NPS contribution tax benefit. Section 80CCD(2) deduction. NPS tax benefit available in new tax regime.

text: Section 80CCD(2) mein employer ka NPS contribution: sarkaari employees ke liye 14%, niji employees ke liye 10% basic+DA tak deduction milta hai. Ye dono Old aur New Tax Regime mein available hai.
textHindi: Section 80CCD(2) mein employer ka NPS contribution: sarkaari employees ke liye 14%, niji employees ke liye 10% basic+DA tak deduction milta hai. Ye dono Old aur New Tax Regime mein available hai.
embedding_hint: Employer NPS contribution tax benefit. Section 80CCD(2) deduction. NPS tax benefit available in new tax regime.

text: Under the New Tax Regime (introduced for FY 2020-21, made default from FY 2023-24), the Section 80CCD(1B) deduction of Rs. 50,000 is NOT available. Section 80CCD(1) deduction for own contribution is also not available under the new regime. The ONLY NPS-related tax benefit available under the New Tax Regime is Section 80CCD(2) — the employer's contribution deduction (14% for government, 10% for private sector employees). Individuals opting for the new tax regime should factor this in when deciding whether NPS still makes sense purely from a tax standpoint. For those with large employer NPS contributions, the new regime can still be attractive. Voluntary NPS contributions (own contributions) offer no deduction under the new regime.
textHindi: New Tax Regime mein 80CCD(1) aur 80CCD(1B) nahi milte. Sirf 80CCD(2) (employer contribution) milta hai. Apna contribution karne par new regime mein koi tax benefit nahi.
embedding_hint: NPS tax benefit under new tax regime. Is 80CCD(1B) available in new tax regime? NPS deduction in new vs old regime.

text: New Tax Regime mein 80CCD(1) aur 80CCD(1B) nahi milte. Sirf 80CCD(2) (employer contribution) milta hai. Apna contribution karne par new regime mein koi tax benefit nahi.
textHindi: New Tax Regime mein 80CCD(1) aur 80CCD(1B) nahi milte. Sirf 80CCD(2) (employer contribution) milta hai. Apna contribution karne par new regime mein koi tax benefit nahi.
embedding_hint: NPS tax benefit under new tax regime. Is 80CCD(1B) available in new tax regime? NPS deduction in new vs old regime.

text: At NPS maturity (age 60 or above), the corpus can be partially withdrawn as a tax-free lump sum. Up to 60% of the total NPS corpus can be withdrawn as a one-time lump sum, and this entire amount is completely tax-free in the hands of the subscriber — no capital gains tax, no income tax. The remaining 40% must mandatorily be used to purchase an annuity plan from a PFRDA-empanelled insurer. The annuity income (monthly pension) received after purchase is, however, fully taxable as 'income from salary' under the applicable income tax slab. If the total corpus at maturity is Rs. 5 lakh or less, the subscriber may withdraw the full amount as a lump sum without any annuity purchase requirement.
textHindi: 60+ par 60% corpus tax-free lumpsum nikal sakte hain. Baaki 40% annuity kharni padti hai. Annuity income par tax lagta hai. Rs. 5 lakh ya kam corpus par poora lumpsum le sakte hain.
source: PFRDA Withdrawal Rules + Income Tax Act
embedding_hint: Is NPS maturity amount tax free? How is NPS taxed at withdrawal? 60% lump sum NPS tax exemption.

text: 60+ par 60% corpus tax-free lumpsum nikal sakte hain. Baaki 40% annuity kharni padti hai. Annuity income par tax lagta hai. Rs. 5 lakh ya kam corpus par poora lumpsum le sakte hain.
textHindi: 60+ par 60% corpus tax-free lumpsum nikal sakte hain. Baaki 40% annuity kharni padti hai. Annuity income par tax lagta hai. Rs. 5 lakh ya kam corpus par poora lumpsum le sakte hain.
source: PFRDA Withdrawal Rules + Income Tax Act
embedding_hint: Is NPS maturity amount tax free? How is NPS taxed at withdrawal? 60% lump sum NPS tax exemption.

text: The monthly pension or income received from an annuity purchased at NPS maturity is fully taxable under the Income Tax Act. It is classified as 'Income from Salary' (for former employees) or 'Income from Other Sources', depending on the individual's status. The tax is computed based on the applicable income tax slab for the year in which the annuity is received. There is no separate exemption for annuity income. Senior citizens (60+) benefit from a higher basic exemption limit, so small annuity amounts may fall below the taxable threshold. No TDS is deducted by the annuity provider if Form 15H is submitted. Planning the annuity amount carefully can minimize tax outgo in retirement.
textHindi: Annuity income par puri tarah income tax lagta hai. Ye salary income manayi jaati hai. Form 15H se TDS rok sakte hain. Senior citizen ko higher exemption milti hai.
embedding_hint: Is NPS annuity income taxable? Tax on monthly pension from NPS. How to avoid TDS on NPS annuity.

text: Annuity income par puri tarah income tax lagta hai. Ye salary income manayi jaati hai. Form 15H se TDS rok sakte hain. Senior citizen ko higher exemption milti hai.
textHindi: Annuity income par puri tarah income tax lagta hai. Ye salary income manayi jaati hai. Form 15H se TDS rok sakte hain. Senior citizen ko higher exemption milti hai.
embedding_hint: Is NPS annuity income taxable? Tax on monthly pension from NPS. How to avoid TDS on NPS annuity.

text: No TDS (Tax Deducted at Source) is deducted on NPS partial withdrawals or lump sum withdrawals if the subscriber submits Form 15G (for non-senior citizens below 60) or Form 15H (for senior citizens aged 60 and above) to the fund house or annuity provider before the withdrawal is processed. Form 15G or 15H is a self-declaration that your total income for the year does not exceed the taxable threshold. If TDS has already been deducted, it can be claimed as a refund when filing the annual Income Tax Return (ITR). Even if TDS is not deducted, you must declare the withdrawal income in your ITR and pay applicable tax if your total income exceeds the exemption limit.
textHindi: NPS nikalne par TDS nahi kataa jata agar Form 15G (60 se kam) ya Form 15H (senior citizen) submit kar diya jaye. Agar TDS kat gaya to ITR mein refund le sakte ho.
embedding_hint: How to avoid TDS on NPS withdrawal. Form 15G 15H for NPS. TDS rules for NPS withdrawal.

text: NPS nikalne par TDS nahi kataa jata agar Form 15G (60 se kam) ya Form 15H (senior citizen) submit kar diya jaye. Agar TDS kat gaya to ITR mein refund le sakte ho.
textHindi: NPS nikalne par TDS nahi kataa jata agar Form 15G (60 se kam) ya Form 15H (senior citizen) submit kar diya jaye. Agar TDS kat gaya to ITR mein refund le sakte ho.
embedding_hint: How to avoid TDS on NPS withdrawal. Form 15G 15H for NPS. TDS rules for NPS withdrawal.

text: NPS Tier I is a long-term pension account with a lock-in until the subscriber reaches age 60. No regular or routine withdrawal is permitted before the age of 60. This lock-in is a fundamental feature of NPS — it ensures the corpus is preserved for retirement. However, two exceptions exist: (1) partial withdrawal under specific circumstances after 3 years of account opening, and (2) premature exit (full exit before 60) under exceptional cases. The lock-in makes NPS distinct from other savings instruments like mutual funds or FDs, and reinforces its purpose as a retirement-specific vehicle. The corpus continues to earn market-linked returns throughout the lock-in period.
textHindi: Tier I ka paisa 60 saal tak lock rehta hai. Normal withdrawal nahi ho sakta. Sirf partial withdrawal (3 saal baad, specific reasons) ya premature exit allowed hai.
embedding_hint: When can I withdraw from NPS? NPS lock-in period. Can I withdraw NPS before 60?

text: Tier I ka paisa 60 saal tak lock rehta hai. Normal withdrawal nahi ho sakta. Sirf partial withdrawal (3 saal baad, specific reasons) ya premature exit allowed hai.
textHindi: Tier I ka paisa 60 saal tak lock rehta hai. Normal withdrawal nahi ho sakta. Sirf partial withdrawal (3 saal baad, specific reasons) ya premature exit allowed hai.
embedding_hint: When can I withdraw from NPS? NPS lock-in period. Can I withdraw NPS before 60?

text: Partial withdrawal from NPS Tier I is permitted after completing 3 years of account opening. The key conditions are: (1) you can withdraw a maximum of 25% of your OWN contributions only — the employer's contributions and returns earned are not included. (2) Partial withdrawal is allowed a maximum of 3 times during the entire tenure of the NPS account before maturity. (3) The withdrawal must be for one of the PFRDA-approved reasons: higher education of self or children, marriage of children, purchase or construction of first home, treatment of critical illness or disability, or skill development/startup activity. Partial withdrawal amounts are tax-free. No annuity purchase is required for partial withdrawals.
textHindi: 3 saal baad aur valid reason ke saath partial withdrawal kar sakte hain. Sirf apna 25% contribution nikal sakte hain. Poori zindagi mein sirf 3 baar allowed hai. Amount tax-free hota hai.
embedding_hint: NPS partial withdrawal rules. Can I withdraw partial amount from NPS? How many times can I withdraw from NPS?

text: 3 saal baad aur valid reason ke saath partial withdrawal kar sakte hain. Sirf apna 25% contribution nikal sakte hain. Poori zindagi mein sirf 3 baar allowed hai. Amount tax-free hota hai.
textHindi: 3 saal baad aur valid reason ke saath partial withdrawal kar sakte hain. Sirf apna 25% contribution nikal sakte hain. Poori zindagi mein sirf 3 baar allowed hai. Amount tax-free hota hai.
embedding_hint: NPS partial withdrawal rules. Can I withdraw partial amount from NPS? How many times can I withdraw from NPS?

text: PFRDA has specified the following valid reasons for partial withdrawal from NPS Tier I before maturity: (1) Higher education of the subscriber or subscriber's children, including for skill development courses. (2) Marriage of the subscriber's children. (3) Purchase or construction of a residential house or flat (first home only; not applicable if already owns property). (4) Treatment of specified critical illnesses such as cancer, kidney failure, heart surgery, stroke, coma, organ transplant, etc., for self or immediate family. (5) Permanent disability due to accident or illness. (6) Setting up a new business or startup. Proof of the specific reason must be submitted to the PoP or CRA when applying for partial withdrawal.
textHindi: Partial withdrawal ke valid reasons: padhai, bacchon ka vivah, ghar khareedna, gambhir bimari, vikalangta, ya nayi company kholna. Sabut dena hoga.
embedding_hint: What are valid reasons to withdraw from NPS? NPS withdrawal for medical emergency. NPS partial withdrawal for home purchase.

text: Partial withdrawal ke valid reasons: padhai, bacchon ka vivah, ghar khareedna, gambhir bimari, vikalangta, ya nayi company kholna. Sabut dena hoga.
textHindi: Partial withdrawal ke valid reasons: padhai, bacchon ka vivah, ghar khareedna, gambhir bimari, vikalangta, ya nayi company kholna. Sabut dena hoga.
embedding_hint: What are valid reasons to withdraw from NPS? NPS withdrawal for medical emergency. NPS partial withdrawal for home purchase.

text: When a subscriber reaches age 60, they can initiate the NPS exit process. At exit, a minimum of 40% of the total NPS corpus must be used to purchase an annuity (pension plan) from a PFRDA-empanelled insurance company. The remaining 60% (or up to 60%) can be withdrawn as a one-time, tax-free lump sum. If the total corpus is Rs. 5 lakh or below, the subscriber is exempt from the annuity requirement and may withdraw the full amount as a lump sum. The subscriber can also defer the lump sum withdrawal or annuity purchase by up to 3 years (until age 63) while staying invested. The exit process is initiated through the CRA portal (npscra.nsdl.co.in) or through the PoP.
textHindi: 60 par minimum 40% annuity kharni padti hai aur maximum 60% tax-free lumpsum nikal sakte hain. Rs. 5 lakh ya kam corpus par poora lumpsum le sakte hain. Exit online ya PoP se hota hai.
embedding_hint: How to exit NPS at 60. NPS maturity withdrawal rules. 60% lump sum 40% annuity NPS.

text: 60 par minimum 40% annuity kharni padti hai aur maximum 60% tax-free lumpsum nikal sakte hain. Rs. 5 lakh ya kam corpus par poora lumpsum le sakte hain. Exit online ya PoP se hota hai.
textHindi: 60 par minimum 40% annuity kharni padti hai aur maximum 60% tax-free lumpsum nikal sakte hain. Rs. 5 lakh ya kam corpus par poora lumpsum le sakte hain. Exit online ya PoP se hota hai.
embedding_hint: How to exit NPS at 60. NPS maturity withdrawal rules. 60% lump sum 40% annuity NPS.

text: If a subscriber wishes to exit NPS before reaching age 60 (premature exit), the rules are significantly stricter. A minimum of 80% of the total corpus must be used to purchase an annuity from a PFRDA-approved insurer. Only 20% of the corpus can be taken as a lump sum. This rule applies to voluntary exit; subscribers who are government employees may have different rules upon resignation. Premature exit is only allowed after completing at least 5 years of NPS membership. If the corpus is Rs. 2.5 lakh or below, the subscriber may withdraw the full amount as a lump sum without purchasing annuity. The 20% lump sum in premature exit is taxable, unlike the 60% tax-free lump sum available at age 60.
textHindi: 60 se pehle nikalna ho to 80% annuity mein lagana padta hai, sirf 20% lumpsum milta hai. 5 saal ki membership zaroori hai. Rs. 2.5 lakh ya kam par poora lumpsum. 20% lumpsum taxable hota hai.
embedding_hint: NPS withdrawal before age 60. Premature exit NPS rules. Can I exit NPS before retirement?

text: 60 se pehle nikalna ho to 80% annuity mein lagana padta hai, sirf 20% lumpsum milta hai. 5 saal ki membership zaroori hai. Rs. 2.5 lakh ya kam par poora lumpsum. 20% lumpsum taxable hota hai.
textHindi: 60 se pehle nikalna ho to 80% annuity mein lagana padta hai, sirf 20% lumpsum milta hai. 5 saal ki membership zaroori hai. Rs. 2.5 lakh ya kam par poora lumpsum. 20% lumpsum taxable hota hai.
embedding_hint: NPS withdrawal before age 60. Premature exit NPS rules. Can I exit NPS before retirement?

text: In the unfortunate event of the NPS subscriber's death, the entire corpus (100%) is paid out to the registered nominee or legal heir. There is no requirement for the nominee to purchase an annuity. The full amount is paid as a tax-free lump sum directly to the nominee. This is a key advantage over the subscriber's own exit rules, where 40% must go into annuity. The nominee must submit a death certificate, their ID proof, and bank account details to the PoP or CRA to initiate the claim. If no nominee was registered, the legal heirs (as per succession law) must provide legal heirship or succession certificate to claim the corpus. Claims are typically processed within 30–45 working days.
textHindi: Subscriber ki mrityu par nominee ko 100% corpus tax-free milta hai. Annuity kharni nahi padti. Nominee ko death certificate, ID proof aur bank details dene padte hain. Claim 30-45 din mein hota hai.
embedding_hint: What happens to NPS when subscriber dies? NPS nominee claim process. NPS death benefit rules.

text: Subscriber ki mrityu par nominee ko 100% corpus tax-free milta hai. Annuity kharni nahi padti. Nominee ko death certificate, ID proof aur bank details dene padte hain. Claim 30-45 din mein hota hai.
textHindi: Subscriber ki mrityu par nominee ko 100% corpus tax-free milta hai. Annuity kharni nahi padti. Nominee ko death certificate, ID proof aur bank details dene padte hain. Claim 30-45 din mein hota hai.
embedding_hint: What happens to NPS when subscriber dies? NPS nominee claim process. NPS death benefit rules.

text: Adding and updating nominees for NPS is important and should be done at the time of account opening and reviewed periodically. The nominee details can be updated online via the CRA portal at npscra.nsdl.co.in by logging in with the PRAN number and IPIN. Multiple nominees can be added, with a specified percentage allocated to each. If you update your marital status, it is advisable to review and update nominee information accordingly. Changes to nominee details are reflected within 5–7 working days. Nominee updates made through the portal are accepted without physical paperwork. In case of minor nominees, a guardian must be specified. Always ensure your nominee's name matches their official ID documents to avoid claim disputes.
textHindi: Nominee account kholte time add karo. Baad mein CRA portal (npscra.nsdl.co.in) se PRAN aur IPIN ke zariye update kar sakte ho. Multiple nominees add ho sakte hain. Update 5-7 din mein hota hai.
embedding_hint: How to add or change nominee in NPS. NPS nominee update online. Multiple nominees in NPS.

textHindi: Nominee account kholte time add karo. Baad mein CRA portal (npscra.nsdl.co.in) se PRAN aur IPIN ke zariye update kar sakte ho. Multiple nominees add ho sakte hain. Update 5-7 din mein hota hai.

text: NPS has two types of accounts: Tier I and Tier II. Tier I is the primary, mandatory pension account that is locked until age 60. All tax benefits — 80CCD(1), 80CCD(1B), 80CCD(2) — apply only to Tier I contributions. You must have a Tier I account before you can open a Tier II account. Tier II is a voluntary, flexible savings account linked to your PRAN. It has no lock-in period — you can withdraw from it at any time. Tier II does not provide any tax deduction for most taxpayers (except Central Government employees under certain conditions). Tier II functions more like a mutual fund savings account, while Tier I is a pure retirement instrument. Both share the same PRAN number.
textHindi: Tier I mandatory pension account hai (60 tak locked). Tier II optional savings account hai (koi lock-in nahi). Tax benefit sirf Tier I par milta hai. Dono ek hi PRAN se chalte hain.
embedding_hint: Difference between NPS Tier 1 and Tier 2. NPS Tier 2 account rules. Should I open NPS Tier 2?

text: Tier I mandatory pension account hai (60 tak locked). Tier II optional savings account hai (koi lock-in nahi). Tax benefit sirf Tier I par milta hai. Dono ek hi PRAN se chalte hain.
textHindi: Tier I mandatory pension account hai (60 tak locked). Tier II optional savings account hai (koi lock-in nahi). Tax benefit sirf Tier I par milta hai. Dono ek hi PRAN se chalte hain.
embedding_hint: Difference between NPS Tier 1 and Tier 2. NPS Tier 2 account rules. Should I open NPS Tier 2?

text: Tier II NPS contributions do NOT qualify for tax deductions under Section 80CCD(1), 80CCD(1B), or Section 80C for most individuals. However, Central Government employees who contribute to Tier II with a lock-in of 3 years can claim a deduction under Section 80C up to Rs. 1.5 lakh. This exception does NOT apply to state government employees, private sector employees, or self-employed individuals. Returns earned in Tier II are treated as normal capital gains and taxed accordingly based on the type of fund (equity, debt) and holding period. There is no special tax treatment for Tier II. This makes Tier II primarily attractive for liquidity and flexibility, not tax saving, unless you are a Central Government employee.
textHindi: Tier II par tax benefit nahi milta aam logon ko. Central Govt employees 3-year lock-in ke saath 80C claim kar sakte hain. Capital gains par normal tax lagta hai.
embedding_hint: NPS Tier 2 tax benefits. Can I get tax deduction on NPS Tier 2? Tier 2 tax rules for government employees.

text: Tier II par tax benefit nahi milta aam logon ko. Central Govt employees 3-year lock-in ke saath 80C claim kar sakte hain. Capital gains par normal tax lagta hai.

text: PRAN stands for Permanent Retirement Account Number. It is a unique 12-digit numeric identifier assigned to every NPS subscriber. A PRAN is issued once per person for a lifetime — you cannot have more than one PRAN. If you accidentally open multiple PRANs, you must close the duplicate immediately by contacting your PoP or the CRA helpline. PRAN is portable across employers, cities, states, and sectors — it follows you wherever you work. PRAN serves both Tier I and Tier II accounts under one number. PRAN is issued within a few working days of completing eNPS registration with Aadhaar OTP or physical KYC at a PoP center. The PRAN card (physical or digital) serves as proof of NPS membership.
textHindi: PRAN ek 12-ank ka unique number hai. Ek baar milta hai aur poori zindagi valid rehta hai. Multiple PRAN nahi khol sakte. Naukri badalne par bhi same PRAN rehta hai.
embedding_hint: What is PRAN in NPS? How is PRAN issued? Can I have two PRANs? PRAN portability across jobs.

text: You can apply for a new PRAN online at the eNPS portal: enps.nsdl.com. The process for Aadhaar-based online enrollment is completely paperless: (1) Visit enps.nsdl.com and click 'New Registration'. (2) Select 'Aadhaar' as the document for eKYC. (3) Enter your Aadhaar number and verify via OTP sent to your Aadhaar-registered mobile. (4) Your personal details (name, DOB, address) are auto-filled from UIDAI. (5) Fill in additional details: bank account, nominee, fund manager, and asset allocation. (6) Make the initial contribution (min Rs. 500 for Tier I). (7) PRAN is generated instantly and the PRAN card is sent to your address. No physical form or bank visit is required for Aadhaar-based registration.
textHindi: PRAN ke liye enps.nsdl.com par jao. Aadhaar OTP se verify karo. Details auto-fill hoti hain. Initial contribution karo aur PRAN turant generate ho jata hai. Koi kaagaz nahi chahiye.
embedding_hint: How to apply for PRAN online. NPS registration process. eNPS portal PRAN application steps.

text: Annuity purchased at NPS maturity or premature exit must be bought from a PFRDA-empanelled insurance company. Currently approved annuity providers include: Life Insurance Corporation (LIC) of India, Bajaj Allianz Life, Birla Sun Life (Aditya Birla Capital), HDFC Life, ICICI Prudential Life, Max Life Insurance, Kotak Mahindra Life, SBI Life, and others. These insurers offer multiple annuity plan options: (1) Life Annuity — pension until subscriber's death. (2) Life Annuity with Return of Purchase Price — pension until death, and the corpus is returned to nominee on death. (3) Joint Life Annuity — pension continues to spouse after subscriber's death. (4) Annuity with Guaranteed Period — pension paid for a fixed period regardless of death.
textHindi: Annuity sirf PFRDA ke approved insurance companies se khareedni padti hai jaise LIC, HDFC Life, ICICI Prudential, etc. Annuity ke kai prakar hain: life annuity, joint life, return of purchase price, etc.
embedding_hint: Which companies provide NPS annuity? Types of annuity available in NPS. Best annuity plan for NPS.

text: uity — pension continues to spouse after subscriber's death. (4) Annuity with Guaranteed Period — pension paid for a fixed period regardless of death. Subscribers should compare rates from different providers before purchasing, as rates vary significantly.

text: Once you purchase an annuity using your NPS corpus, it is a PERMANENT and IRREVOCABLE decision. You cannot change the annuity provider after purchase. You cannot change the annuity type or plan after purchase. You cannot surrender the annuity and get your money back (unlike some insurance policies). The annuity rate (monthly pension amount) is fixed at the time of purchase based on the corpus amount and the then-prevailing rates offered by the insurer. Because this decision is irreversible, it is strongly advisable to: (1) Compare annuity rates across multiple PFRDA-approved providers before deciding. (2) Consider your spouse's longevity and financial needs when choosing plan type. (3) Not rush the decision — PFRDA allows up to 3 years to defer the annuity purchase after reaching age 60.
textHindi: Ek baar annuity khareed lene ke baad provider ya type change nahi kar sakte. Ye permanent decision hai. Khareedne se pehle sabhi providers ke rates compare karo. 60 ke baad 3 saal tak wait kar sakte ho.
embedding_hint: Can I change NPS annuity provider after purchase? Is NPS annuity reversible? How to choose best NPS annuity.

text: To open an NPS account, the following KYC documents are mandatory: (1) Identity Proof: Aadhaar card is the primary identity proof; if Aadhaar is unavailable, PAN card or passport can be used. (2) PAN Card: Mandatory for all NPS accounts, as it is required for tax reporting. (3) Bank Account Proof: A cancelled cheque or the first page of the bank passbook showing account holder name, account number, IFSC code, and bank branch details. (4) Photograph: A recent passport-size color photograph. For online/eNPS registration using Aadhaar OTP, the photograph is captured digitally and physical documents are not required. For offline (PoP) registration, self-attested copies of all documents must be submitted.
textHindi: NPS kholne ke liye: Aadhaar/PAN, PAN card, cancelled cheque ya passbook, aur photo zaroori hai. eNPS par Aadhaar OTP se sab digital ho jata hai.
embedding_hint: What documents are needed to open NPS account? KYC requirements for NPS. NPS documents checklist.

text: NPS kholne ke liye: Aadhaar/PAN, PAN card, cancelled cheque ya passbook, aur photo zaroori hai. eNPS par Aadhaar OTP se sab digital ho jata hai.

text: NRI applicants have additional document requirements over and above the standard KYC documents: (1) Valid Indian Passport with at least 6 months remaining validity. (2) Overseas Address Proof: utility bill, bank statement, or lease agreement from the country of residence, not older than 3 months. (3) Country of Residence Certificate or Tax Residency Certificate if claiming treaty benefits. (4) FEMA compliance declaration — a signed self-declaration that the applicant is compliant with FEMA regulations for overseas investments. (5) NRE or NRO bank account details for contribution and withdrawal routing. All documents must be self-attested. For documents in a foreign language, a certified English translation is required.
textHindi: NRI ke liye extra documents: valid Indian passport, videshi address proof, FEMA declaration, aur NRE/NRO bank account details zaroori hain.
embedding_hint: Documents required for NRI to open NPS. NRI NPS account document list. FEMA declaration for NPS NRI.

text: NPS subscribers can check their account balance, view transaction history, and download account statements online. The primary portal for this is the CRA (Central Recordkeeping Agency) portal at npscra.nsdl.co.in. To log in, use your 12-digit PRAN number and your IPIN (Internet Personal Identification Number). The IPIN is provided during PRAN registration; if forgotten, it can be reset via OTP. The portal shows current NAV (Net Asset Value), unit balance in each asset class (E, C, G, A), total corpus value, contribution history, and fund manager performance. Annual Consolidated Account Statements (CAS) are also emailed to the registered email ID. Statements are accepted by the Income Tax Department for 80CCD proof.
textHindi: NPS balance check karne ke liye npscra.nsdl.co.in par PRAN aur IPIN se login karo. NAV, contribution history, fund performance sab dekhne milta hai. CAS email par aati hai.
embedding_hint: How to check NPS balance. NPS account statement download. NPS CRA portal login.

text: NPS account details such as bank account, nominee, mobile number, email, and address can be updated online via the CRA portal (npscra.nsdl.co.in) after logging in with PRAN and IPIN. Most updates do not require physical forms and are processed within 5–7 working days. For grievances, complaints, or queries, NPS has a dedicated 24/7 toll-free helpline: 1800-222-080. Grievances can also be raised via the online complaint mechanism on the CRA portal. For fund manager-specific issues, the respective fund manager's customer care can be contacted. If your IPIN is forgotten or locked, you can reset it via OTP on the CRA portal. For PRAN card reissue, apply through the portal or your PoP.
textHindi: Bank, nominee, mobile, email — sab CRA portal se update kar sakte ho. Complaint ke liye 1800-222-080 (24/7 toll-free) ya CRA portal pe complaint daalo. IPIN bhool gaye to OTP se reset karo.
embedding_hint: NPS helpline number. How to update bank details in NPS. NPS complaint or grievance process.

text: Bank, nominee, mobile, email — sab CRA portal se update kar sakte ho. Complaint ke liye 1800-222-080 (24/7 toll-free) ya CRA portal pe complaint daalo. IPIN bhool gaye to OTP se reset karo.

text: EPFO (EPF) and NPS are both government-backed retirement savings schemes but differ fundamentally. EPF is mandatory for salaried employees earning basic salary up to Rs. 15,000 per month; NPS is mandatory only for central/state government employees, and voluntary for the private sector. EPF offers a fixed, government-declared interest rate (8.25% for 2023-24); NPS returns are market-linked and depend on fund performance — potentially higher or lower. EPF corpus is fully tax-free on withdrawal after 5 years of service; NPS at maturity: only 60% lump sum is tax-free, 40% must go into a taxable annuity. Both qualify for 80C deduction (EPF up to Rs. 1.5 lakh). NPS additionally offers the exclusive Rs. 50,000 deduction under 80CCD(1B) that EPF does not.
textHindi: EPFO mandatory hai under Rs. 15,000 salary ke liye. NPS govt ke liye mandatory, private ke liye optional. EPF fixed return (8.25%), NPS market-linked. EPF 5 saal baad fully tax-free. NPS mein sirf 60% tax-free.
source: EPFO & PFRDA Guidelines + Income Tax Act
keywords: should i choose nps or epf
embedding_hint: EPF vs NPS comparison. Which is better EPF or NPS? Difference between EPFO and NPS.

text: Both qualify for 80C deduction (EPF up to Rs. 1.5 lakh). NPS additionally offers the exclusive Rs. 50,000 deduction under 80CCD(1B) that EPF does not. For the highest earners, NPS + EPF together maximize tax savings.

text: PFRDA has empanelled several fund managers for NPS. Approved pension fund managers include: SBI Pension Funds, LIC Pension Fund, HDFC Pension Fund, ICICI Prudential Pension Fund, Kotak Mahindra Pension Fund, UTI Retirement Solutions, Aditya Birla Sun Life Pension Fund, and Max Life Pension Fund. Subscribers can choose one fund manager and switch to another once per year at no cost. NPS offers four asset classes: (E) Equity — maximum 75% allocation allowed (higher risk, higher potential return); (C) Corporate Bonds — moderate risk, fixed income; (G) Government Securities — lowest risk, stable; (A) Alternative Investment Funds — max 5%, high-risk alternative assets.
textHindi: Fund managers: SBI, LIC, HDFC, ICICI, Kotak, UTI, Aditya Birla, Max Life. Saal mein ek baar free switch. Asset classes: E (equity 75%), C (bonds), G (govt), A (alternative 5%). Auto Choice me equity khud kam hoti hai.
keywords: nps asset class E C G A
embedding_hint: NPS fund managers list. How to switch NPS fund manager. NPS equity allocation. Active vs Auto choice in NPS.

text: moderate risk, fixed income; (G) Government Securities — lowest risk, stable; (A) Alternative Investment Funds — max 5%, high-risk alternative assets. Subscribers can either self-manage allocation (Active Choice) or opt for Auto Choice (Lifecycle Fund), which automatically reduces equity exposure as the subscriber ages closer to 60.
